title: trifolium leibergii
intro: Trifolium leibergii, also known as Leiberg's clover, is a rare species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the northwestern region of North America, particularly in the state of Washington. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. Leiberg's clover can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, offering various health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing pain, and promoting wound healing.
